text: ‘The magician’ is a translation, not of Bar-Jesus, but of Elymas, a name which Bar-Jesus had adopted.
The name of Paul is here mentioned for the first time, probably because it was the name he used when travelling in Gentile countries.
‘You who worship the true God’, that is, Gentile adherents of the Jewish faith, like Cornelius (10.2).

St Paul is probably reckoning as four hundred and fifty years the space of time between the patriarchs and Josue. Some early authorities give a different reading, according to which the space of four hundred and fifty years is reckoned between Josue and Samuel.
‘For our posterity’; a few early authorities have ‘for us, their children’. The quotation is from Ps. 2.7.
Ps. 15.10. Cf. St Peter’s argument in Ac. 2.27.
Hab. 1.5; there are some differences in the Hebrew.
